name: Castillo de Urbel — circular by La Piedra
name_original: Castillo de Urbel — circular por La Piedra
region_intro: Hiking around Basconcillos Del Tozo offers access to a unique karstic landscape within Spain's Burgos province, part of the UNESCO World Geopark of Las Loras. The region is characterized by spectacular karstic relief and river valleys, providing a diverse environment for exploration. This topography includes notable features such as dolines, sinkholes, and a network of underground rivers. Best hiking trails around Basconcillos Del Tozo The most popular hiking route is Camino del Molino - from Hoyos del Tozo — circular through the Hoces del Alto Ebro y Rudrón Natural Park, an 8.9 miles (14.3 km) trail that takes 4 hours 3 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the natural park's varied terrain. Another top favourite among local hikers is Castillo de Urbel — circular by La Piedra, a moderate 5.5 miles (8.8 km) path. This trail offers views of the surrounding landscape and historical elements. Local hikers also love the Route from Sobrepeña to Lorilla (PR-S 38) — circular through the villages of Valderredible, a 7.0 miles (11.2 km) trail leading through the villages of Valderredible, often completed in about 3 hours 21 minutes. Hiking around Basconcillos Del Tozo is defined by karstic relief, river valleys, and unique geological formations like dolines and waterfalls. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy paths to more challenging routes. The routes in Basconcillos Del Tozo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 20 reviews. More than 100 hikers have used komoot to explore Basconcillos Del Tozo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Basconcillos Del Tozo?
A: There are around 15 hiking trails in Basconcillos Del Tozo, offering a variety of experiences through its unique karstic landscape and river valleys. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Basconcillos Del Tozo?
A: Yes, Basconcillos Del Tozo offers at least one easy hiking trail, perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed walk. The majority of routes are moderate, providing accessible options for various fitness levels.

Q: What kind of natural attractions can I expect to see on the trails?
A: The trails in Basconcillos Del Tozo showcase a unique karstic landscape, featuring spectacular geological formations, river valleys, and waterfalls. You can discover natural monuments like the striking blue waters of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2577310" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozo Azul de Covanera&lt;/a&gt;, the serene &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4382544" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Fuentona&lt;/a&gt;, and the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3066377" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Cañada Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. The region is part of the UNESCO World Geopark of Las Loras, highlighting its significant natural interest.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Basconcillos Del Tozo?
A: Yes, many of the trails in Basconcillos Del Tozo are circular, allowing you to start and end your hike at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1046678966/camino-del-molino-from-hoyos-del-tozo-circular-through-the-hoces-del-alto-ebro-y-rudron-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino del Molino&lt;/a&gt;, which explores the Hoces del Alto Ebro y Rudrón Natural Park, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1047058710/castillo-de-urbel-circular-by-la-piedra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Urbel&lt;/a&gt; route.

Q: Are the hiking trails in Basconcillos Del Tozo suitable for families with children?
A: Basconcillos Del Tozo offers several moderate trails that can be suitable for families, especially those with older children. The diverse landscapes, including waterfalls and unique geological features, provide engaging points of interest. Consider routes that are not too long or have significant elevation gain for younger hikers.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Basconcillos Del Tozo?
A: Many trails in natural areas like Basconcillos Del Tozo are generally dog-friendly, but it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or livestock. Be mindful of local regulations and ensure your dog is well-behaved around other hikers and nature.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Basconcillos Del Tozo?
A: The best time for hiking in Basconcillos Del Tozo is typically during the short, dry summers. While winters are cold, long, and can be snowy, offering a different kind of beauty, the warmer months provide more comfortable conditions to explore the region's karstic landscapes and river valleys.

Q: Are there any viewpoints with panoramic views in the area?
A: Yes, the region boasts stunning viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3932476" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valcabado Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, located in the Covalagua Natural Area, offers spectacular vistas of the Valderredible Valley and is also an excellent spot for stargazing due to minimal light pollution.

Q: Where can I find parking for the hiking trails?
A: Many trailheads in Basconcillos Del Tozo, especially those near villages like Hoyos del Tozo or starting points for popular routes, typically have designated parking areas. It's advisable to check the specific route details on komoot for parking information before you set out.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Basconcillos Del Tozo?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 20 reviews. Hikers often praise the unique karstic relief, the serene river valleys, and the diverse natural features like waterfalls and geological formations that make each trail a discovery.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural points of interest along the hiking routes?
A: Beyond natural beauty, the region offers historical elements. For instance, near the Valcabado Viewpoint, you can find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3932476" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozo de los Lobos&lt;/a&gt; (Wolf's Well), a historic stone structure once used for trapping wolves. Some routes also pass through charming villages, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1176557971/route-from-sobrepena-to-lorilla-pr-s-38-circular-through-the-villages-of-valderredible" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route from Sobrepeña to Lorilla&lt;/a&gt;, offering a glimpse into local life.

Q: Can I go wild swimming in Basconcillos Del Tozo?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2577310" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozo Azul de Covanera&lt;/a&gt; is a notable natural monument in the Rudrón valley, famous for its strikingly blue water. It's accessible via a beautiful path and is a popular spot for swimming and even underwater exploration for experienced divers.
tips:
Urbel Castle, also known as Urbel Castle of the Castle, is a medieval fortress located in the province of Burgos. This castle is located in a strategic position on a hill, which allowed it to control the valley of the Urbel River and the trade routes of the region.

Built in the 9th century, the castle has gone through several phases of reconstruction and restoration over the centuries. Although it is currently in ruins, remains of its walls, towers and other defensive structures can be seen. The natural environment that surrounds it and the panoramic views from the castle make it a place of both historical and tourist interest.

La Piedra is a small town in the municipality of Basconcillos del Tozo, in the province of Burgos, Castile and Leon. This place is known for its ashlar stone buildings, typical of the region, and its natural surroundings that invite exploration and enjoyment of the landscape.
La Piedra is located about 48 kilometers from Burgos, making it an accessible destination for a day trip.
